title: Closed Again, Still Flowing

mood.label: Uneasy calm
mood.summary: Three days after it reopened, Iran called the Strait of Hormuz shut again while strikes kept falling in Lebanon and Ebola climbed in central Africa — yet the oil kept moving and a string of quiet miracles, a child's sight handed back, mangroves coming home, held the day just shy of neutral.

style.genre: downtempo neo-soul / dusk-electronic — a sampled Rhodes-ish electric piano laying ambiguous chords over a slow, dusty AkaiLinn kit with a soft clap, a deep sine sub and a bowed-string drone underneath, a sampled vibraphone tracing a sparse, hopeful topline with tape-echo; Eb melodic minor's raised sixth and seventh keeping the minor third unresolved — uneasy, but refusing to go dark

pattern: stack( s("bd ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ bd ~, ~ ~ ~ ~ cp ~ ~ ~, ~ ~ hh ~ ~ ~ hh ~").bank("AkaiLinn").gain(0.6).room(0.16), s("hh*8").bank("AkaiLinn").gain(0.16).pan(sine.range(0.38,0.62).slow(9)).hpf(7000), note("<ef2 ef2 bf1 c2>").s("sine").lpf(380).gain(0.5).attack(0.01).release(0.45), note("[ef3,gf3,bf3,d4] ~ [c3,ef3,gf3,bf3] ~ [af3,c4,ef4] ~ [bf3,d4,f4] ~").s("sawtooth").attack(0.7).sustain(0.7).release(2.6).lpf(sine.range(480,1500).slow(16)).gain(0.2).room(0.46).rsize(3), note("ef4 ~ gf4 bf4 ~ d5 c5 ~ af4 ~ bf4 ~ gf4 ~ ~ ~").s("piano").gain(0.34).lpf(3000).delay(0.3).delaytime(0.333).delayfeedback(0.32).pan(0.44).room(0.3).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.16).pan(0.6)), note("~ ~ bf4 ~ ~ ~ d5 ~ ~ ~ ~ ef5 ~ ~ c5 ~").s("vibraphone").gain(0.32).room(0.5).rsize(4).delay(0.24).delaytime(0.375).pan(0.56), note("<ef3 bf2 af2 c3>").s("sawtooth").attack(1.5).release(3.2).lpf(820).gain(0.13).room(0.6) )
sampleSources: Strudel default library (sampled piano, VCSL vibraphone, Akai/Roland drum machines)

lines: Three days open and the gate swung shut again,
guard boats on the radio waving off the ships at ten.
Down in southern Lebanon the dark gave back its dead,
a ceasefire only hours old already turning red.
Closed again — but the oil still finds the sea,
fifty-five hulls and seventeen million, moving easy.
Closed again, and the floor's still holding tight,
we're uneasy in the morning, not undone by the night.
A worse thing climbs in Congo where the count won't slow,
but a six-year-old in England saw the whole room glow.
A shot that thins the odds, the mangroves coming home —
good news kept its small light burning through the gloam.
Closed again — but the oil still finds the sea,
fifty-five hulls and seventeen million, moving easy.
Closed again, and the floor's still holding tight,
we're uneasy in the morning, not undone by the night.
